title: Digify Active: B2B Marketing Agency Simulation
context: A team-based academic project focused on building and testing a B2B marketing agency from scratch. The objective was to simulate a realistic go-to-market process, combining strategic positioning, UX/UI, SEO, paid acquisition, and performance analysis.
problem: Many SME fitness businesses lack structured digital marketing capabilities and rely on fragmented acquisition channels. This created an opportunity to design a marketing service tailored to their operational constraints.
actions: Market Positioning & Offer Design: Developed value proposition and pricing logic for a service-based B2B offering.
Customer Journey & Conversion Funnel: Designed conversion-focused funnel and built landing pages in HubSpot.
UX/UI Design & SEO: Prototyped in Figma, applied foundational on-page SEO, and adapted UX to HubSpot constraints.
Paid Acquisition: Launched Google Ads (Search & Display) with a limited budget to test acquisition approaches.
Performance Analysis: Tracked CTR, CPA, and funnel behavior in HubSpot to identify drop-off points.
myContribution: Designed conversion funnel structure and customer journey logic. Co-developed landing pages (copywriting and HubSpot implementation). Contributed to SEO research and participated in campaign analysis, connecting performance data with optimization decisions.
result: Conversion depended more on clarity of value proposition than traffic volume. Search campaigns outperformed Display. The simplified UX had a measurable impact on efficiency, resulting in real leads generated during the simulation.

title: AI Startup | Market Opportunity & Repositioning
context: A short-term consulting engagement with an early-stage AI startup in image generation and dataset infrastructure. Still in product development, the focus was on strategic direction setting rather than performance optimization.
problem: Given the absence of an established market presence, the primary objective was to identify a viable market opportunity: Where and how should the company position its core technology to achieve differentiation and long-term viability?
actions: Week 1 — Analytical Phase: Conducted market landscape analysis, competitive benchmarking, and demand driver assessment.
Explored unconventional applications beyond the original use case to identify gaps in high-regulation industries.
Week 2 — Strategy & Recommendations: Developed new positioning logic scaling away from horizontal applications toward vertical specialization.
Designed a scalable SaaS-based business model and a phased go-to-market approach.
myContribution: Responsible for market and industry analysis, competitive benchmarking, identification of strategic opportunity areas, and the development of the core positioning logic and go-to-market framework.
result: Identified that addressing a structural data gap in a specialized industry offered higher potential than horizontal AI use cases. Delivered a strategic report and presented to the CEO, establishing a viable, data-backed growth direction.
